result.stdout:
Description:
  Acuit.MarkdownToHtml Tool

Usage:
  Acuit.MarkdownToHtml [options]

Options:
  -b, --base <base>               The base path to which input, exclude, and output parameters are relative. If this is not specified, the current directory is used.
  -i, --input <input> (REQUIRED)  Input Markdown file(s). Only relative file paths can be specified, relative to the base parameter. Multiple parameter values are allowed. File glob patterns (* and **) are supported.
  -x, --exclude <exclude>         Input Markdown file exclusion(s). Only relative file paths can be specified, relative to the base parameter. Multiple parameter values are allowed. File glob patterns (* and **) are supported. Do not prefix with !.
  -t, --template <template>       HTML template file. This must contain a [content] placeholder for the converted content and can contain a [title] placeholder for the title. This can be an absolute path or a path relative to the base parameter.
  -o, --output <output>           Output HTML file, only valid when a single input file is matched. When multiple files are matched or when this is not specified, output is written to a .html file in the same folder as the .md file. This can be an absolute path or a path relative to the base parameter.
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information
  --version                       Show version information

  -x, --exclude <exclude>         Input Markdown file exclusion(s). Only relative file paths can be specified, relative to the base parameter. Multiple parameter values are allowed. File glob patterns (* and **) are supported. Do not prefix with !.
  -t, --template <template>       HTML template file. This must contain a [content] placeholder for the converted content and can contain a [title] placeholder for the title. This can be an absolute path or a path relative to the base parameter.
  -o, --output <output>           Output HTML file, only valid when a single input file is matched. When multiple files are matched or when this is not specified, output is written to a .html file in the same folder as the .md file. This can be an absolute path or a path relative to the base parameter.
  -?, -h, --help                  Show help and usage information
